content: Curiosity-driven autonomous agents can use a dynamically evolving, human-like semantic MemoryBank to self-manage their working memory capacity limits and outperform existing active learning agent memory SOTA, by only pulling high-reward unexpected observations into the active working set based on intrinsic curiosity signals. This MemoryBank acts as a long-term curiosity filter that prunes low-information stale memories and prioritizes novel patterns that align with the agent’s current exploratory goals.
